# Demographics: Rural aging and population decline

**Cambridge is caught in a storm buffeting rural school districts throughout New York**

Population is declining in rural areas throughout New York.

[go to the popfc (population forecast) project home\donboyd5\Documents\python_projects\popfc and get population history data there for washingon, warren, saratoga, and rensselaer counties and do an indexed plot of total population for (I think) 1995–2020, with 2000 = 100. It would be great if you can add Bennington and Rutland counties in vermont, too. You will have to get those data.

Make a second plot of the 4 NY counties with the same data for 2020–2035, with 2020 = 100.

You do not need to write interpretie text about these plots - I will do taht.

then do a table for washington county showing the annual components of change for 2020-2035 -- years in rows, components in columns births, deaths, domestic migration, international migration, total change

]

The main reasons for this decline are:

-  Declines in the number of births, which is a statewide trend
-  Out-migration of younger adults, leading to an aging population
-  Economic factors that make rural areas less attractive for new residents
